subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI) + Norwegian official (SSB, Norges Bank, NBIM, UDI) + Norwegian/Nordic press.
Anti-paradox-of-plenty: major oil/gas wealth is saved and managed prudently via the world's largest sovereign wealth fund - NOT a resource curse.
The long Russia border / High North is a real geopolitical tail; source-lean low on the load-bearing numbers (Norwegian primaries).

economic.notes: Extraordinary fiscal strength via the sovereign wealth fund, prudently managed resource wealth, and a credible central bank.

fiscal_state.notes: The sovereign wealth fund (~$2T+, the world's largest), continued petroleum surpluses, effectively no net government debt, and a disciplining fiscal rule. (NBIM; regjeringen.no)

monetary_independence.notes: A fully independent, credible Norges Bank with an own floating krone. (Norges Bank)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The krone is not a reserve currency and is small/volatile (a recurring imported-inflation driver). (Norges Bank; IMF)

inflation.notes: ~3.4% (above the 2% target), with Norges Bank tightening to converge medium-term. (SSB; Norges Bank)

banking_stability.notes: A robust, highly profitable system well above capital/liquidity rules; high household debt and commercial-real-estate exposure the watch-items. (Norges Bank FSR 2026)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Diversified resource strength (oil/gas, record seafood, shipping); Europe's #1 gas supplier post-Russia, with stable, predictable policy. (Norwegianpetroleum.no; SSB)

institutional.notes: Among the world's strongest and most stable institutions; the world's freest press.

rule_of_law.notes: WJP Rule of Law #2/143; an independent judiciary. (WJP 2025)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A highly capable, well-resourced, digitally advanced administration. (OECD)

political_social.notes: Very stable consensus democracy with very high trust; the fund pre-funds future fiscal/pension pressure.

political_stability.notes: A stable consensus democracy; the 2025 election returned a Labour-led red-green bloc, with coalition management the only modest drag. (Storting)

civil_liberties.notes: Very strong protections across speech, assembly, and privacy. (WJP; RSF)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: EEA free movement (not EU); a lawful, selective route for non-EU (quota, salary floor, PR at 3y); dual citizenship since 2020. (UDI)

social_cohesion.notes: Very high interpersonal and institutional trust, low inequality. (OECD; SSB)

demographics.notes: Ageing, but immigration-fed labour supply and the fund pre-fund future fiscal/pension pressure. (SSB)

geopolitical.notes: A strong NATO founding member with a real Russia-border/High North tail.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO founding member rearming toward 3.5%; EEA (not EU) the only caveat. (NATO)

conflict_involvement.notes: A ~198 km Russia border and NATO's High North front line, with Russian gray-zone activity (GPS jamming, suspected subsea sabotage); a real tail, no direct conflict. (NATO; Defense News)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: EU-aligned sanctions, a fully open capital account. (regjeringen.no)

physical_practical.notes: Excellent healthcare, infrastructure, and safety, with near-universal catastrophe insurance and relatively favorable climate.

climate.notes: A high-latitude net beneficiary near/mid-term (top readiness, hydro-rich, milder winters, Arctic access), but AMOC slowdown and warming-overshoot temper the long outlook. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6; Burke et al.)

healthcare.notes: Universal, with strong access/outcomes and high long-term-care capacity. (OECD)

disaster_insurance.notes: A mandatory natural-perils pool bundled with fire insurance gives near-universal catastrophe coverage. (Finance Norway)

summary: Extraordinary fiscal strength (the world's largest sovereign wealth fund, no net debt) with prudently managed resource wealth - the anti-resource-curse - alongside top-tier institutions, the world's freest press, very high social trust, and excellent healthcare, infrastructure, and safety. The main drag is a real Russia-border/High North geopolitical tail; the krone is small and volatile, and inflation runs a bit above target.
